caption: Transport
heading: Goods movement services

structure.text: Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods between countries?
Movement Reference Number (MRN) — text input revealed under 'Yes – add MRN now'
Will the transport use the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)?
Save and review (amend/review-flow variant of the primary button — replaces 'Save and continue' when the page is reached via a Change link from the Review notification page)
structure.evidence: page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:49-51 (btnSaveAndReview 'Save and review'); driven in the amend flow at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1330-1333 (changeGoodsMovementServices → radioCommonTransitNo → Save and review)

govukComponents.usage: CTC question (3 options, with conditional MRN reveal) and GVMS question (2 options)
Groups each radio question with its legend
Movement Reference Number (MRN) — revealed under CTC 'Yes – add MRN now'
Label for the MRN text input
Hint under the CTC legend, and format hint under the MRN input
Expandable help: 'What is the CTC?' and 'What is the GVMS?'

'Save and return to hub' (name=save-and-return-button) and 'Save and continue' (name=button-save-and-continue). A third primary-button label 'Save and review' replaces 'Save and continue' in the amend/review flow (inferred — page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:49-51).
Three lists inside the CTC/GVMS Details help content: one bulleted (the UK / the EU / other CTC countries) and two numbered

'Cancel and return to hub' link (href .../overview, the hub), plus four external gov.uk guidance links opening in new tabs inside the CTC/GVMS Details help content: 'Find out more about using transit to move goods (opens in new tab)', 'Find out which ports use the GVMS (opens in new tab)', 'Register for the GVMS (opens in new tab)', 'Get a goods movement reference (GMR) (opens in new tab)'
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 136 (5 a.govuk-link in main)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Positioning modifier that offsets the Back link so it sits alongside the breadcrumb/account bar
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-standard IPAFFS-specific modifier class layered on the standard govuk-back-link; purely a layout tweak for the legacy chrome
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-back-link with no custom modifier; the new CDP app has its own layout and will not need this offset

fields.name: ctc-question
fields.label: Are you using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) to move goods between countries?
fields.hint: If yes, you must provide the Movement Reference Number (MRN) that will have been generated by NCTS.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 136 (ids ctc-question-yes / ctc-question-yes-add-later / ctc-question-no). Corroborated by page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:13-19 (labels 'Yes – add MRN now' / 'Yes – add MRN later') and :26-31 (ids #ctc-question-yes / #ctc-question-no). Test-driven values 'yes' | 'yes-later' | 'no' at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:579 (type ChedPCtcSelection) and the fill logic at :615-622.
fields.notes: The page object also exposes radioCommonTransitYes = getByLabel('Yes', { exact: true }) (GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:9-11), which cannot match any of the three labels above — it targets a plain 'Yes' radio. The main BCP CHED-P flow drives this exact-'Yes' locator (ched-p-workflows.ts:488), whereas the GVMS-port/CUC flow drives the three-option 'Yes – add MRN now' locator (ched-p-workflows.ts:615-616). This strongly implies a SECOND rendering of the CTC question — a simple Yes/No — exists for some transport/BCP combinations. The trace captured only the three-option variant. See openQuestions.

fields.name: ncts-mrn
fields.label: Movement Reference Number (MRN)
fields.hint: It is 18 characters, starting with 2 numbers, a 2-letter country code then 14 letters and numbers. For example, 24GB123456789AB012.
fields.conditionalOn: ctc-question = 'Yes – add MRN now' (govuk-radios__conditional reveal; hidden by default)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 136 (input#ncts-mrn inside govuk-radios__conditional--hidden). Corroborated by page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:21-23 (inputMrn = getByRole('textbox', { name: 'MRN' })), filled only on the 'yes' branch at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:616-617.
fields.notes: The tests fill this via generateNctsMrn() (utils/reference-utils.ts:5-9), which produces the format YYGB<9 digits>AB012 (e.g. 24GB123456789AB012) — matching the hint's '2 numbers + 2-letter country code + 14 alphanumeric' shape. This is the value shape the tests exercise, not a server-observed constraint; the actual server-side MRN validation (length/pattern) was never exercised and remains a gap.

fields.name: gvms-question
fields.label: Will the transport use the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)?
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 136 (ids gvms-question / gvms-question-2). Corroborated by page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:38-43 (radioTransportUse Yes/No within group 'Will the transport use the'); test-driven Yes/No at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:631-634.
fields.notes: The rendered No radio id is gvms-question-2 (standard govuk-frontend sequential id) per the trace. The page object's #gvms-question-no locator (GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:35-37) is marked //todo and does not match the rendered id — trace value wins; treat #gvms-question-no as stale test scaffolding, not a requirement.

behaviours.name: conditional-page-and-question-presence
behaviours.description: The CTC and GVMS questions (and by extension this whole page) are not unconditional. The CHED-P workflow guards each question in a try/catch and only clicks Save if at least one question rendered, with the explaining comment: 'GVMS ports may show, hide, or reorder these questions depending on the transport method and CTC/GVMS combination.' So depending on the entry BCP / means-of-transport, the page may present both questions, one, or neither — and may reorder them.
behaviours.evidence: workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:604-643 (selectChedPGoodsMovementServices: comment at :605-607, per-question try/catch waitFor at :612-638, guarded Save at :640-642). Same defensive pattern in workflows/notification/ched-a-workflows.ts:395-431.

validationMessages.message: A validation message for a missing CTC or GVMS selection was not observed in this trace (happy-path run, both radios answered) and no test asserts one.
validationMessages.trigger: Likely submitting with no radio selected, or 'Yes – add MRN now' with an empty/invalid MRN
validationMessages.evidence: not observed in traces; no expect() on any GMS error string across tests/notification/**/*.spec.ts (only radioCommonTransitNo.toBeVisible() presence assertions, e.g. tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-date-validation.spec.ts:22)

validationMessages.message: No server-side MRN format/length validation message was observed or asserted. Tests supply only well-formed MRNs (generateNctsMrn); an invalid/empty MRN under 'Yes – add MRN now' was never exercised.
validationMessages.trigger: Selecting 'Yes – add MRN now' and submitting an empty or malformed MRN
validationMessages.evidence: not observed in traces; no MRN error assertion in the QA repo (utils/reference-utils.ts:5-9 only generates valid values)

openQuestions: The 'required' attribute is false on all controls (server-side validation, not HTML required). Whether CTC and GVMS answers are mandatory, and the exact validation copy for missing answers or an invalid/empty MRN, was not exercised in this happy-path trace and is not asserted by any test — mine an error-state trace or confirm with a human.

The MRN format/length is enforced by hint only (18 chars, 2 digits + 2-letter country code + 14 alphanumeric, e.g. 24GB123456789AB012); no maxlength attribute is set. The exact server-side MRN validation message is a gap. Tests only ever supply valid MRNs via generateNctsMrn() (utils/reference-utils.ts:5-9).

CTC question — TWO renderings appear to exist: the three-option 'Yes – add MRN now' / 'Yes – add MRN later' / 'No' variant (captured in the trace), and a plain 'Yes' / 'No' variant that the main BCP CHED-P flow drives via getByLabel('Yes', exact) (page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:9-11 vs :13-19; ched-p-workflows.ts:488 vs :615-616). Which BCP / transport combinations produce which variant — and whether the new IUU app needs both — is a question for a human.

Conditional page/question presence: the CHED-P and CHED-A workflows treat both questions as optionally-rendered and even reorderable ('GVMS ports may show, hide, or reorder these questions', ched-p-workflows.ts:605-607). Confirm the exact rule that governs when the Goods movement services page (and each question) is shown for IUU — traces only captured the both-questions-shown case.

The primary button label is 'Save and continue' on the standard journey but 'Save and review' when the page is reached via a Change link from the Review notification page (page-objects/notification/GoodsMovementServicesPage.ts:49-51; ched-p-workflows.ts:1330-1333). Confirm the new app's amend-flow button labelling.

Details help copy for 'What is the CTC?' and 'What is the GVMS?' captured verbatim (contains external gov.uk guidance links opening in new tabs); confirm whether the new app carries this same help content.
Page is 100% GOV.UK Design System components (only non-standard item is the back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs positioning modifier) — cleanly rebuildable in govuk-frontend.
